message:

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kcp-1074-rename-workloadcluster-to-synctarget",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kcp: Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget",
+    "description": "Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kcp deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kcp version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp\nhelm list -n kcp 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kcp installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kcp configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp\nkubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp\n```\nWorkloadCluster is a bit misleading and has some overlap with other APIs out there, naming wise. A more appropriate name is something like SyncTarget, because the only thing we're using the API for is as a scheduling target."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget",
+        "description": "let's move forward with SyncTarget, we can change it if we need to.  \n\nAre you able to work on this in 0.6?\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp\nkubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "let's move forward with SyncTarget, we can change it if we need to.  \n\nAre you able to work on this in 0.6?",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
